words.def: the fact that somebody/something is able to do something
words.example: People with the disease may lose their ability to communicate.

words.def: in or to a foreign country
to take willingly something that is offered; to say ‘yes’ to an offer, invitation, etc.
words.example: He asked me to marry him and I accepted.

words.def: an unpleasant event, especially in a vehicle, that happens unexpectedly and causes injury or damage
to succeed in reaching a particular goal, status or standard, especially by making an effort for a long time
from one side to the other side
words.example: It's too wide. We can't swim across.

words.def: used in speaking to emphasize a fact or a comment, or that something is really true
fully grown or developed
words.example: preparing young people for adult life

words.def: a thing that helps you to be better or more successful than other people
an unusual, exciting or dangerous experience, journey or series of events
a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service
the activity and industry of advertising things to people on television, in newspapers, on the internet, etc.
an opinion or a suggestion about what somebody should do in a particular situation
later in time; afterwards
words.example: That was in 1996. Soon after, I heard that he'd died.

words.def: opposing or disagreeing with somebody/something
used to express surprise, pleasure or sympathy, or when you disagree with somebody
the mixture of gases that surrounds the earth and that we breathe
words.example: Let's go out for some fresh air.

words.def: living; not dead
completely
words.example: She was dressed all in white.

words.def: to let somebody/something do something; to let something happen or be done
words.example: allow somebody/something to do something, His parents won't allow him to stay out late.

words.def: not quite
words.example: I like almost all of them.

words.def: without any other people
words.example: not alone in, Tom is not alone in finding Rick hard to work with.

words.def: forward
words.example: I was just walking along singing to myself.

words.def: before now or before a particular time in the past
used for introducing a statement that makes the main statement in a sentence seem surprising
words.example: Although the sun was shining, it wasn't very warm.

words.def: surrounded by somebody/something; in the middle of somebody/something
belonging to a period of history that is thousands of years in the past
words.example: Archaeologists are excavating the ruined temples of this ancient civilization.

words.def: the joint connecting the foot to the leg; the narrow part of the leg just above the ankle joint
used to emphasize an adjective or adverb in negative sentences or questions, meaning ‘at all’
words.example: He wasn't any good at French.

words.def: used instead of somebody in negative sentences and in questions after if/whether, and after verbs such as prevent, forbid, avoid, etc.
words.example: Is there anybody who can help me?

words.def: used when adding something to support an idea or argument
words.example: It's too expensive and anyway the colour doesn't suit you.

words.def: used in negative sentences and in questions instead of somewhere
a set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building
a piece of software that you can download to a device such as a smartphone or tablet, for example to look up information or to play a game
words.example: You first need to install the app on your device.

words.def: to start to be seen
words.example: Three days later a rash appeared.

words.def: the way that somebody/something looks on the outside; what somebody/something seems to be
to make a formal request, usually in writing, for something such as a job, a loan, permission for something, a place at a university, etc.
words.example: You should apply in person/by letter.

words.def: the art and study of designing buildings
part of a place, town, etc., or a region of a country or the world
words.example: to improve access to services in rural areas

words.def: to speak angrily to somebody because you disagree with them
a conversation or discussion in which two or more people disagree, often angrily
approximately
used when you are comparing two people or things, or two situations
words.example: You're as tall as your father.

words.def: sleeping
words.example: I waited until they were all fast asleep (= sleeping deeply).

words.def: having a rank below that of a senior person and helping them in their work
a person who competes in sports
an act of using violence to try to hurt or kill somebody
words.example: Five people were killed in the attacks that took place last night.

words.def: used for asking people to listen to something that is being announced
words.example: Attention, please! Passengers for flight KL412 are requested to go to gate 21 immediately.

words.def: pleasant to look at, especially in a sexual way
the group of people who have gathered to watch or listen to something (a play, concert, somebody speaking, etc.)
words.example: The audience was/were clapping for 10 minutes.

words.def: a person who writes books or the person who wrote a particular book
to prevent something bad from happening
very bad or unpleasant
the details of a person’s family, education, experience, etc.
without skill or care
a game played especially in the US by two teams of nine players, using a bat and ball. Each player tries to hit the ball and then run around four bases before the other team can return the ball.
if one thing is based on another, it uses it or is developed from it
words.example: The movie is based on a real-life incident.

words.def: a game played by two teams of five players, using a large ball which players try to throw into a high net hanging from a ring
a seed, or pod containing seeds, of a climbing plant, eaten as a vegetable. There are several types of bean and the plants that they grow on are also called beans.
meat that comes from a cow
an alcoholic drink made from malt with hops added to give it taste. There are many types of beer.
at an earlier time; in the past; already
words.example: You should have told me so before.

words.def: the time when something starts; the first part of an event, a story, etc.
words.example: A story has to have a beginning, middle, and end.

words.def: the way that somebody behaves, especially towards other people
to be in the right or suitable place
words.example: Where do these plates belong (= where are they kept)?

words.def: a long narrow piece of leather, cloth, etc. that you wear around the middle part of your body
of the most excellent type or quality
words.example: That's the best movie I've ever seen!

words.def: in the space or period of time separating two or more points, objects, etc. or two dates, events, etc.
words.example: The house was near a park but there was a road in between.

words.def: the time when a baby is born; the process of being born
words.example: at birth, The baby weighed three kilos at birth.

words.def: a small flat dry cake for one person, usually sweet, and baked until hard
rather; to some extent
words.example: These trousers are a bit tight.

words.def: empty, with nothing written, printed or recorded on it
words.example: Sign your name in the blank space below.

words.def: pale gold in colour
the red liquid that flows through the bodies of humans and animals
words.example: He lost a lot of blood in the accident.

words.def: when a liquid boils or when you boil it, it is heated to the point where it forms bubbles and turns to steam or vapour
words.example: The water was bubbling and boiling away.

words.def: any of the hard parts that form the skeleton of the body of a human or an animal
words.example: He survived the accident with no broken bones.

words.def: a set of printed pages that are fastened inside a cover so that you can turn them and read them
words.example: His desk was covered with piles of books.

words.def: feeling tired and impatient because you have lost interest in somebody/something or because you have nothing to do
words.example: There was a bored expression on her face.

words.def: to take and use something that belongs to somebody else, and return it to them at a later time
words.example: borrow something, Can I borrow your umbrella?

words.def: a person who is in charge of other people at work and tells them what to do
words.example: I'll ask my boss if I can have the day off.

words.def: in the lowest, last or furthest place or position
a deep round dish with a wide open top, used especially for holding food or liquid
the organ inside the head that controls movement, thought, memory and feeling
words.example: The human brain is a complex organ.

words.def: a short period of time when you stop what you are doing and rest, eat, etc.
words.example: She was on her lunch break.

words.def: a structure that is built over a road, railway, river, etc. so that people, vehicles, etc. can cross from one side to the other
words.example: We crossed the bridge over the River Windrush.

words.def: full of light; shining strongly
extremely clever or impressive
that has been damaged or injured; no longer whole or working correctly

an object made of short stiff hairs (called bristles) or wires set in a block of wood or plastic, usually attached to a handle. Brushes are used for many different jobs, such as cleaning, painting and tidying your hair.
